text.title: pytest Fixtures: Scopes, Cleanup, and Composition
text.summary: Fixture scope rules, yield-based teardown, and conftest.py organization for pytest test suites.

rule.statement: Default to `scope="function"` for all fixtures. Widen scope only after profiling shows cost AND the fixture state is provably immutable during the test run.
rule.rationale: Wider scopes leak mutable state between tests causing order-dependent failures. The failure manifests as "tests pass alone, fail in suite" — one of the hardest bugs to diagnose.

section.title: Yield-Based Cleanup

rule.statement: Use yield-based fixtures (not return) for any fixture that acquires a resource. Cleanup after yield always runs, even if the test raises.
rule.rationale: Return-based fixtures cannot clean up on test failure. Yield-based fixtures execute cleanup in a finally block, preventing resource leaks.

example.note: Database fixture uses savepoint rollback: each test gets a clean database state without DROP/CREATE overhead.

section.reference: SQLAlchemy session fixture with savepoint rollback

rule.statement: Place project-wide fixtures in `tests/conftest.py`; module-specific fixtures in `tests/{module}/conftest.py`. Never dump everything in the root conftest.
rule.rationale: A monolithic root conftest loads all fixtures for every test, including expensive fixtures that are irrelevant to 90% of tests. Per-directory conftests scope cost appropriately.
